title: Lasthenia debilis
taxonomy: Lasthenia debilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lasthenia, which is a group of daisy-like plants native to the western United States and Mexico. Within the Asteraceae family, Lasthenia debilis is part of the tribe Madieae. The plant is further classified as Lasthenia debilis (Nutt.) Greene, a synonym for Lasthenia debilis Nutt. The species is also known as the weakstem tarweed or weakstem lasthenia.
benefits: Lasthenia debilis, commonly known as slender goldfields, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves, flowers, and stems are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Some studies suggest that Lasthenia debilis extracts may have antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic effects, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. The plant's traditional uses also include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, although its efficacy for these uses has not been scientifically proven. Further research is necessary to fully understand the health benefits and potential applications of Lasthenia debilis.
properties: Lasthenia debilis, also known as weakstem goldfields, is a plant native to California and is known for its potential therapeutic properties. It contains flavonoids, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, that may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant's leaves and stems may be used to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic benefits of Lasthenia debilis, but it has been traditionally used by some communities for its medicinal properties. More studies are necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
parts: Lasthenia debilis, also known as fringe-parsley or dune tansy, has been used in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves and stems are considered medicinal parts. intro: Lasthenia debilis, also known as the slender goldfields, is a small annual plant native to the western United States and Canada. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Lasthenia debilis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lasthenia debilis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support joint health and well-being.
